constraints_data.text: Response must start with "Encoding.." followed by compressed version on new line

prompt: When I set the alarm for 04:00, am I truly preparing to climb—or am I just trying to outrun the quiet thoughts that come when the gear is packed and the fire is out?

prompt: Is the emergency beacon I checked just a tool—or is it a promise I made to someone I haven’t met yet, someone who might one day search for my name in the snow?

prompt: What if the mountain doesn’t want me to summit again? What if it’s been waiting, patient and ancient, for me to finally choose to walk away?

prompt: Did my teammate’s laugh earlier today echo differently because of the thin air—or because something in me has begun to shift, quietly, irreversibly?

prompt: When I whisper thanks, am I speaking to something sentient in the mountain… or am I just trying to convince myself that I still belong here?

prompt: If I never climbed another peak after this, would I still feel this peace—or is it only the danger that makes the stillness so sacred?

prompt: What does it mean to be “ready” for sleep when your body is still wired for altitude, your ears still ringing from the wind, and your soul still clinging to the edge of the world?

prompt: Could the stars above me be the same ones that watched my first climb—and if they are, do they know how much I’ve changed since then?

prompt: When I finally return home, will I miss the cold more than the warmth? The fear more than the calm? And will I be brave enough to admit it?

prompt: If I died tonight, right here under this sky, would anyone find my notes in my pack—and would they understand why I chose to be here, alone, with the wild?
